cases.name: arithmetic_eval
cases.description: Bottom-up arithmetic evaluator: tokens -> int, rules -> sum/product
cases.grammar:
start: expr
            expr: term "+" term
                | term
            term: factor "*" factor
                | factor
            factor: NUMBER
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: json_value_builder
cases.description: JSON-like value builder: strings, numbers, arrays, objects
cases.grammar:
start: value
            ?value: string | number | array | object | "true" | "false" | "null"
            string: ESCAPED_STRING
            number: NUMBER
            array: "[" (value ("," value)*)? "]"
            object: "{" (pair ("," pair)*)? "}"
            pair: ESCAPED_STRING ":" value

            NUMBER: /\-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?/
            ESCAPED_STRING: "\"" /[^"]*/ "\""
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: token_normalizer_upper
cases.description: Token normalizer: uppercase all NAME tokens, rules identity
cases.grammar:
start: greeting+
            greeting: NAME PUNCT
            NAME: /[a-zA-Z]+/
            PUNCT: /[!.?]/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: alias
cases.description: Rule alias: 'a -> alias_a' renames the tree node
cases.grammar:
start: item+
            item: "a" -> item_a
                | "b" -> item_b
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: transparent_rule
cases.description: Transparent rule (_inner) inlines into parent
cases.grammar:
start: _inner+
            _inner: WORD
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: expand1
cases.description: ?rule (expand1) collapses single-child rules
cases.grammar:
start: wrapper
            ?wrapper: inner
            inner: WORD
            WORD: /[a-z]+/

cases.name: filtered_punctuation
cases.description: Punctuation tokens (string literals) are filtered by default
cases.grammar:
start: item ("," item)*
            item: WORD
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: keep_all_tokens
cases.description: keep_all_tokens=True preserves punctuation tokens
cases.grammar:
start: item ("," item)*
            item: WORD
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: maybe_placeholders_true
cases.description: maybe_placeholders=True: bracket-optional [X] missing -> None placeholder
cases.grammar:
start: WORD [NUMBER]
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: maybe_placeholders_false
cases.description: maybe_placeholders=False: bracket-optional [X] missing -> absent
cases.grammar:
start: WORD [NUMBER]
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: discard_token
cases.description: Token callback returns Discard, removing it from parent's children
cases.grammar:
start: item+
            item: WORD NOISE?
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NOISE: /[!@#]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: discard_rule
cases.description: Rule callback returns Discard, removing it from parent's children
cases.grammar:
start: (keep | drop)+
            keep: WORD
            drop: NUMBER
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: default_rule_handler
cases.description: __default__ handles rules without explicit callbacks
cases.grammar:
start: alpha beta
            alpha: WORD
            beta: NUMBER
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: default_token_handler
cases.description: __default_token__ handles tokens without explicit callbacks
cases.grammar:
start: item+
            item: WORD | NUMBER
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            NUMBER: /[0-9]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: callback_order_trace
cases.description: Traces the exact bottom-up callback order across rules and tokens
cases.grammar:
start: first second
            first: A B
            second: C
            A: "a"
            B: "b"
            C: "c"
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: unless_keyword_retype
cases.description: A string-literal keyword terminal (IF) retypes a NAME match via Lark's `unless` mechanism; the token's retyped type drives callback dispatch — IF tokens hit the IF method, plain identifiers hit NAME
cases.grammar:
start: word+
            word: NAME | IF
            IF: "if"
            NAME: /[a-z]+/
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: ignored_token_no_callback
cases.description: A %ignore'd NAMED terminal (WS) never reaches the tree, so its callback never fires even though a WS method is defined — ignored tokens do not surface to the transformer
cases.grammar:
start: WORD+
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            WS: /\s+/
            %ignore WS

cases.name: discard_token_maybe_placeholders
cases.description: A token callback returns Discard for a PRESENT optional [B]: the child is removed entirely (no None hole left behind), distinct from the maybe_placeholders None inserted only for an ABSENT optional
cases.grammar:
start: A [B] C
            A: "a"
            B: "b"
            C: "c"
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: discard_token_maybe_placeholders_absent
cases.description: Same grammar as discard_token_maybe_placeholders but with [B] ABSENT: maybe_placeholders inserts a None at B's position and the B token callback never fires — pins the sibling-position semantics
cases.grammar:
start: A [B] C
            A: "a"
            B: "b"
            C: "c"
            %ignore /\s+/

cases.name: template_expanded
cases.description: A parameterized template `sep{item, COMMA}` expands but dispatches on the template SOURCE name (`sep`); the named COMMA punctuation is kept (a named terminal surfaces) while default-handled rules nest
cases.grammar:
start: sep{item, COMMA}
            sep{x, s}: x (s x)*
            item: WORD
            WORD: /[a-z]+/
            COMMA: ","
            %ignore /\s+/
